fullDisplayName: Wait and See Hammer 236/214 Lost Thunder
rules:
You can use this card only if you go second, and only on your first turn.

Discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

When the Psychic Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for a Basic Grass Pokémon or a Grass Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Put 2 cards from your hand in the Lost Zone. If you do, draw a card.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

During this turn, your Lightning Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

You may play 2 Custom Catcher cards at once.
• If you played 1 card, draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.
• If you played 2 cards, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. (This effects works one time for 2 cards.)

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, the attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to cost Colorless less.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

The Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from the attacks of your opponent's Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pelagic Blade
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Pelagic Blade during your next turn.

attacks.name: Lost Purge-GX
attacks.effect: Put your opponent's Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it in the Lost Zone. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Let's Snuggle & Fall
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dream Fear-GX
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Your opponent shuffles that Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Snowy Wind
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Sublimation-GX
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Ultra Beast, it is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Break Buster-GX
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dusty Ruckus
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Basic Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Lay the Smackdown-GX
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sonic Wing
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Intercept-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Plasma Fists
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Full Voltage-GX
attacks.effect: Attach 5 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cure Stream
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Brinicles-GX
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bursting Burn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

attacks.name: Mind Blown
attacks.effect: Put any amount of Fire Energy attached to your Pokémon in the Lost Zone. This attack does 50 damage for each card put in the Lost Zone in this way.

attacks.name: Burst-GX
attacks.effect: Discard 1 of your Prize cards. If it's an Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lava Flow
attacks.effect: Discard any amount of basic Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Burning Magma-GX
attacks.effect: Discard the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Sensitive Blade
attacks.effect: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Breeze Away-GX
attacks.effect: Put any number of your Pokémon in play and all cards attached to them into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mach Cut
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leaf Cyclone
attacks.effect: Move a Grass Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Jungle Heal-GX
attacks.effect: Heal all damage from each of your Pokémon that has any Grass Energy attached to it. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Triple Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 3 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Wrap-GX
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Draw a card. Then, draw 2 cards for each other Whitney in your discard pile.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon with 60 HP or less, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

You can play this card only if 1 of your Psychic Pokémon was Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.

Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose 2 cards you find there. Your opponent shuffles those cards into their deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for a Fairy Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Draw 2 cards. Then, flip a coin. If heads, if you played this Kahili from your hand, put this card into your hand instead of the discard pile. If you have no cards in your deck, you can't play this card.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Each player shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 4 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Choose a Pokémon Tool or Special Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon, or any Stadium card in play, and put it in the Lost Zone.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

This card provides Colorless Energy.

The Pokémon this card is attached to can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

Draw a card. Then, draw 2 cards for each other Whitney in your discard pile.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

fullDisplayName: Wait and See Hammer 192/214 Lost Thunder
rules:
You can use this card only if you go second, and only on your first turn.

Discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

The attacks of Lightning Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) cost Lightning less.

Whenever any player plays an Item or Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to this Stadium card.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

When the Psychic Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

You may discard any number of cards from your hand. Then, draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand. If you can't draw any cards in this way, you can't play this card.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

You may discard any number of cards from your hand. Then, draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand. If you can't draw any cards in this way, you can't play this card.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon with 60 HP or less, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon with 60 HP or less, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for a Basic Grass Pokémon or a Grass Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for a Basic Grass Pokémon or a Grass Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

You can play this card only if 1 of your Psychic Pokémon was Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.

Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose 2 cards you find there. Your opponent shuffles those cards into their deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Choose 1 of your Pokémon, and then flip 2 coins. For each heads, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

You may play 2 Mixed Herbs cards at once.
• If you played 1 card, remove a Special Condition from your Active Pokémon.
• If you played 2 cards, heal 90 damage and remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon. (This effects works one time for 2 cards.)

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for a Fairy Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

You can play this card only if your opponent has exactly 3 Prize cards remaining.

Prevent all damage done to your Ultra Beasts by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

Put 2 cards from your hand in the Lost Zone. If you do, draw a card.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Once during each player's turn, that player may heal 60 damage and remove all Special Conditions from 1 of their Grass Pokémon.

Whenever any player plays an Item or Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to this Stadium card.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

Draw 2 cards. Then, flip a coin. If heads, if you played this Kahili from your hand, put this card into your hand instead of the discard pile. If you have no cards in your deck, you can't play this card.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Once during each player's turn, that player may discard a Fire Energy card from their hand. If they do, they draw 3 cards.

Whenever any player plays an Item or Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to this Stadium card.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Prevent all damage done to the Fairy Pokémon this card is attached to by attacks from your opponent's Dragon Pokémon-GX and Dragon Pokémon-EX.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Prevent all damage done to the Fairy Pokémon this card is attached to by attacks from your opponent's Fighting Pokémon-GX and Fighting Pokémon-EX.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Prevent all damage done to the Fairy Pokémon this card is attached to by attacks from your opponent's Psychic Pokémon-GX and Psychic Pokémon-EX.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Prevent all damage done to the Fairy Pokémon this card is attached to by attacks from your opponent's Grass Pokémon-GX and Grass Pokémon-EX.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Choose a Pokémon Tool or Special Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon, or any Stadium card in play, and put it in the Lost Zone.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

During this turn, your Lightning Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

You may play 2 Custom Catcher cards at once.
• If you played 1 card, draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.
• If you played 2 cards, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. (This effects works one time for 2 cards.)

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, the attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to cost Colorless less.

The Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from the attacks of your opponent's Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Put 3 Pokémon that have "Alolan" in their names from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Heat Beak
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Giganticannon
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon evolved during this turn, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Within its beak, its internal gas ignites, explosively launching seeds with enough power to pulverize boulders.
By bending its beak, it can produce a variety of calls and brand itself a noisy nuisance for its neighbors.

attacks.name: Send Back
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon feeds on berries, whose leftover seeds become the ammunition for attacks it fires off from its mouth.

attacks.name: Peck Off
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon feeds on berries, whose leftover seeds become the ammunition for attacks it fires off from its mouth.
It changes its shading to match its surroundings so it can sneak up on prey. Only its belly patterns stay fixed.

attacks.name: Tongue Smack
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It changes its shading to match its surroundings so it can sneak up on prey. Only its belly patterns stay fixed.

attacks.name: Rainbow Burn
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each type of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It will reveal itself before a pure-hearted Trainer by shining its bright, rainbow-colored wings.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pelagic Blade
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Pelagic Blade during your next turn.

attacks.name: Lost Purge-GX
attacks.effect: Put your opponent's Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it in the Lost Zone. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Milk Cannon
attacks.effect: Reveal any number of Moomoo Milk cards in your hand. This attack does 60 damage for each card you revealed in this way.

flavorText: Most people raise it for its milk, but it's quite tough and strong, so it's also well suited for battle.

attacks.name: Stunning Likeness
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. You may use the effect of a Supporter card you find there as the effect of this attack.

attacks.name: Tail Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It draws symbols all over the place to mark its territory. In towns with many Smeargle, the walls are covered in graffiti.

attacks.name: Mystifying Horns
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Enhanced Horns
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The curved antlers change the flow of air to create a strange space where reality is distorted.
Current studies show it can evolve into an incredible eight different species of Pokémon.

rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
flavorText: With its astonishing capacity for metamorphosis, it can get along with anything. It does not get along well with its fellow Ditto.

attacks.name: Powerful Slap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its fluffy fur coat acts as a sensor, enabling it to read the feelings of people and Pokémon.

attacks.name: Healing Pirouette
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sympathetic Slap
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Not only are these Pokémon fast runners, they're also few in number, so anyone who finds one must be lucky indeed.

attacks.name: Dream Away
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck.

attacks.name: Wonder Shine
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The guardian deity of Poni, it can control water. People say it can create pure water that will wash away any uncleanness.
As it flutters about, it scatters its strangely glowing scales. Touching them is said to restore good health on the spot.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Let's Snuggle & Fall
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dream Fear-GX
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Your opponent shuffles that Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dream's Touch
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Asleep, your opponent shuffles all Energy from it into their deck.

flavorText: It emits flickering spores that cause drowsiness. When its prey succumb to sleep, this Pokémon feeds on them by sucking in their energy.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: As it drowses the day away, it nourishes itself by sucking from tree roots. It wakens at the fall of night, wandering off in search of a new tree.
Some of Ribombee's pollen puffs are highly nutritious. They are sometimes sold as supplements.

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Myriads of Cutiefly flutter above the heads of people who have auras resembling those of flowers.

attacks.name: Bright Horns
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Bright Horns during your next turn.

rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
flavorText: When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.

attacks.name: Wonder Ray
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by any Pokémon that has an Ability.

flavorText: Although this Pokémon is not especially rare, its glittering, jewel-draped body draws attention from people.

attacks.name: Zzzap Touch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It uses its tail to absorb electricity from power plants or from outlets in houses, and then it fires the electricity from its whiskers.

attacks.name: Brilliant Search
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Sensitive Ray
attacks.effect: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It has the power to predict the future. Its power peaks when it is protecting its Trainer.

attacks.name: Disarming Voice
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It has a psychic power that enabled it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

attacks.name: Beckon
attacks.effect: Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

attacks.name: All Out
attacks.effect: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 130 more damage.

flavorText: More timid than Snubbull, this Pokémon is doted on by young people amused at the contrast between its looks and its attitude.

attacks.name: Make a Mess
attacks.effect: Discard up to 2 Trainer cards from your hand. This attack does 20 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Its growls make its opponents uneasy. This laid-back Pokémon tends to sleep half the day.

attacks.name: Polka-Dot Search
attacks.effect: Look at the top 8 cards of your deck and attach any number of Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The bubble-like pattern on its stomach helps it camouflage itself when it's in the water.
The tip of its tail is filled with oil that is lighter than water, so it acts as a float.

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Charmed Slap
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card that has "Fairy Charm" in its name attached to it, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: As it inhales, it expands…and expands…and expands. Wigglytuff compete to see which one can inflate itself the most.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Jigglypuff posses a vocal range that exceeds 12 octaves, but each individual's singing skill depends on its own effort.

attacks.name: Snowy Wind
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Sublimation-GX
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Ultra Beast, it is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its mechanized body is merely a vessel. Its true self is its Soul-Heart, an artificial soul.

attacks.name: Break Buster-GX
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Metal Arms
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: It has a body and heart of steel. It worked with its allies to punish people when they hurt Pokémon.

attacks.name: Knock Over
attacks.effect: You may discard any Stadium card in play.

attacks.name: Mountain Munch
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

attacks.name: Turn Back Time
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an evolved Pokémon, devolve it by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

attacks.name: Special Blow
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Once it has identified an enemy, this Pokémon smashes it mercilessly with pincers hard as steel.

attacks.name: Iron Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: It chews its way through boulders with its sturdy jaws. Its eyes can see in the darkness underground.

attacks.name: Thorny Eruption
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Lost Blast
attacks.effect: Put this Pokémon and all cards attached to it in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: It remains immovably rooted to its tree. It scatters piece of its hard shell to drive its enemies away.

attacks.name: Digging Dash
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: These Pokémon are cherished in the Alola region, where they are thought to be feminine deities of the land incarnate.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its golden hairs function as sensors. It pokes them out of its burrow to monitor its surroundings.

attacks.name: Dusty Ruckus
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Basic Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Lay the Smackdown-GX
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: With its black fur, it blends into the darkness. It bides its time, and when prey appears, this Pokémon goes for its threat, and then eats it.

attacks.name: Empty Threat
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 less damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It looks down on everyone other than itself. Its preferred tactics are sucker punches and blindside attacks.

attacks.name: Spoil the Fun
attacks.effect: If you go second, this attack does 60 more damage during your first turn.

flavorText: When its delicate pride is wounded, or when the gold coin on its forehead is dirtied, it flies into a hysterical rage.

attacks.name: Diamond Gate
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Supporter card and a Stadium card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Although this Pokémon is not especially rare, its glittering, jewel-draped body draws attention from people.
Its shell is as hard as sheet rock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

attacks.name: Second Strike
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has 3 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.
Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Triple Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If you are enchanted by its smooth, dance-like kicks, you may get a closer experience with one than you'd like.

attacks.name: Rolling Spin
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Rolling Spin attack does 70 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The longer and bigger its tusks, the higher its rank in its herd. The tusks take long to grow.

attacks.name: Last Resort
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It is strong despite its compact size. It can easily pick up and carry an adult human on its back.

attacks.name: Reply Strongly
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn while it was your Active Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Apparently, the larger the green parts of this Pokémon, the more collectors value it. It's a particular favorite among elderly people.
It usually lives underground. It searches for food while boring its way through the ground at 50 miles per hour.

attacks.name: Turning Point
attacks.effect: If you have exactly 3 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It stores hundreds of liters of poisonous liquid inside its body. It is one of the organisms known as UBs.

attacks.name: Eye Opener
attacks.effect: Look at your face-down Prize cards.

flavorText: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.

attacks.name: Nightcap
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if your opponent has exactly 2 Prize cards remaining. Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

attacks.name: Void Tentacles
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: One of the Ultra Beasts, it's unclear whether or not this Pokémon is sentient, but sometimes it can be observed behaving like a young girl.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It plunges the poison spike on its head into its prey. When the prey has weakened, Mareanie deals the finishing blow with its 10 tentacles.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Miracle Harmony
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Sing attack. This attack does 10 damage for each heads to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feels of those who hear it.

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.effect: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Vortex of Pain
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on all of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: The spirits burned up in its ominous flame lose their way and wander this world forever.

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It arrives near the moment of death and steals spirit from the body.

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.effect: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

attacks.name: Spirit Juggling
attacks.effect: Discard any number of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Grave robbers who mistake them for real coffins and get too close end up trapped inside their bodies.

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.effect: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

attacks.name: Sonic Wing
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Intercept-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shadow Impact
attacks.effect: Put 4 damage counters on 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.

attacks.name: Quick Hunt
attacks.effect: If you go first, you can use this attack on your first turn. Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This Pokémon is feared. When its gemstone eyes begin to glow with a sinister shine, it's believed that Sableye will steal people's spirits away.

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: A most peculiar Pokémon that somehow appears in a Poké Ball when a Nincada evolves.

attacks.name: Get Lost
attacks.effect: Put 2 cards from your opponent's discard pile in the Lost Zone.

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: Its tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent by biting.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it live quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.
Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.
Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.
Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

attacks.name: Allure
attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It unleashes psychic power from the orb on its forehead. When its power is exhausted, the orb grows dull and dark.

attacks.name: Energy Gaze
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. If you find any Energy cards there, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: They say that it stays still and quiet because it is seeing both the past and future at the same time.

attacks.name: Lost March
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon, except ◇ (Prism Star) Pokémon, in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: It usually forages for food on the ground but may, on rare occasions, hop onto branches to peck at shoots.

attacks.name: Plasma Fists
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Full Voltage-GX
attacks.effect: Attach 5 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flashing Bolt
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Flashing Bolt during your next turn.

flavorText: It confuses its enemies by flying too quickly for the eye to follow. It has a hair-trigger temper but forgets what made it angry an instant later.

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Nuzzle Shot
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Nuzzle attack to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It uses its tail to absorb electricity from power plants or from outlets in houses, and then it fires the electricity from its whiskers.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a Lightning Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.
They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.
Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

attacks.name: Overshort
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you discarded a Pokémon Tool card in this way, this attack does 40 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

attacks.name: Lost Voltage
attacks.effect: If you have any Lightning Energy cards in the Lost Zone, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: This rough Pokémon stores energy inside its body, then sweeps across the land, shooting off electricity.

attacks.name: Split Bomb
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The tail's tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

attacks.name: Signal Beam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If its coat becomes fully charged with electricity, its tail lights up. It fires hair that zaps on impact.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It stores lots of air in its soft fur, allowing it to stay cool in summer and warm in winter.
It stores lots of air in its soft fur, allowing it to stay cool in summer and warm in winter.

attacks.name: Salvage
attacks.effect: Shuffle 4 Item cards from your discard pile into your deck.

attacks.name: Signal Beam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lives in the depths beyond the reach of sunlight. It flashes lights on its antennae to communicate with others of its kind.

attacks.name: Electrocharge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: High-Voltage Knuckle
attacks.effect: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When it gets excited, it thumps its chest. With every thud, thunder roars and electric sparks shower all around.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Electricity leaks from it in amounts far greater than the amount of electricity it eats.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Follow the Wound
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It stuns its prey with psychokinesis and then grinds them to mush with its strong teeth. Even Shellder's shell is no match for it.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venom Fever
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 damage for each damage counter on that Pokémon.

flavorText: Those attacked by Toxapex's poison will suffer intense pain for three days and three nights. Post-recovery, there will be some aftereffects.

attacks.name: Spike Cannon
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It's found crawling on beaches and seafloors. The coral that grows on Corsola's head is as good as a five-star banquet to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hypno Splash
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its singing voice is its chief weapon in battle. This Pokémon's Trainer must prioritize the daily maintenance of its throat at all costs.

attacks.name: Captivate
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It cares deeply for its companions. When its Trainer is feeling down, it performs a cheery dance to try and help.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can control water bubbles. It practice diligently so it can learn to make big bubbles.

attacks.name: Disarming Voice
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It's famous for being a hard worker.

attacks.name: Field Crush
attacks.effect: If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, discard it.

attacks.name: Freezing Flames
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached to it, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

attacks.name: Resolute Claws
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-GX or a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Blizzard Burn
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: It freezes its breath to create fangs and claws of ice to fight with. Cold northern areas are its habitat.

attacks.name: Secondary Chills
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only on your first turn. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Their snot is a barometer of health. When healthy, their snot is sticky and the power of their ice moves increases.

attacks.name: Cure Stream
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Brinicles-GX
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aurora Gain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Said to be the embodiment of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.
While elegantly swimming in the sea, it ignores Remoraid that cling to its fins seeking food scraps.

attacks.name: Happy Delivery
attacks.effect: Choose any number of your Benched Pokémon that don't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to them. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It has a generous habit of sharing its food with people and Pokémon, so it's always scrounging around for more food.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: These Pokémon were once near extinction due to poaching. Following protective regulations, there is now an overabundance of them.

attacks.name: Memory Melt
attacks.effect: Look at your opponent's hand and put a card you find there in the Lost Zone.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is so famed for its intellect that a proverb still persists in some regions: "When in doubt, ask Slowking."

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Alolan home cooking involves drying Slowpoke tails and then simmering them into a salty stew.
In hot weather, this Pokémon makes ice shards with its six tails and sprays them around to cool itself off.

attacks.name: Bursting Burn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

attacks.name: Mind Blown
attacks.effect: Put any amount of Fire Energy attached to your Pokémon in the Lost Zone. This attack does 50 damage for each card put in the Lost Zone in this way.

attacks.name: Burst-GX
attacks.effect: Discard 1 of your Prize cards. If it's an Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: With fiery breath of more than 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, they viciously threaten any challenger. The females protect the pride's cubs.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: They set off on their own from their pride and live by themselves to become stronger. These hot-blooded Pokémon are quick to fight.

attacks.name: V-Beatdown
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Basic Pokémon in play.

flavorText: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being's entire body will be overflowing with power.

attacks.name: Lava Burn
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Heat Bazooka
attacks.effect: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck.

flavorText: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Eruption
attacks.effect: Each player discards the top card of their deck. This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.

flavorText: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Attack Operation
attacks.effect: If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: The flames it breathes when angry contain toxins. If they cause a burn, it will hurt forever.

attacks.name: Team Hunt
attacks.effect: Draw a card for each of your Houndour in play.

flavorText: To corner prey, they check each other's location using barks that only they can understand.

attacks.name: Lava Flow
attacks.effect: Discard any amount of basic Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Burning Magma-GX
attacks.effect: Discard the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn't keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

attacks.name: Lost Flame
attacks.effect: Put 2 Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur together to cause huge explosions.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Be careful if it turns its back during battle. It means that it will attack with the fire on its back.
It has a timid nature. If it is startled, the flames on its back burn more vigorously.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It has a timid nature. If it is startled, the flames on its back burn more vigorously.

attacks.name: Assisting Heater
attacks.effect: You may attach a Fire Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

attacks.name: Heavy Punch
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: The guardian deity of Ula'ula is a lazy Pokémon. It commands plants to immobilize its foes and then deals them a savage blow with its horns.

attacks.name: Leaf Wallop
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Leaf Wallop attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: They inhabit mountainous regions. The leader of the herd is decided by a battle of clashing horns.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: If it has sunshine and water, it doesn't need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back.

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Sensitive Blade
attacks.effect: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Breeze Away-GX
attacks.effect: Put any number of your Pokémon in play and all cards attached to them into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

attacks.name: Commanding Queen
attacks.effect: If you have 4 or fewer Grass Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its abdomen is a honeycomb for grubs. It raises its grubs on honey collected by Combee.

attacks.name: Bee March
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Combee and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It collects and delivers honey to its colony. At night, they cluster to form a beehive and sleep.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Because it moves so quickly, it sometimes becomes unseeable. It congregates around tree sap.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It can sometimes live underground for more than 10 years. It absorbs nutrients from the roots of trees.
It violently flutters its wings to scatter toxic dust when attacked. It becomes active after sunset.

attacks.name: Cocoon Collector
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 4 in any combination of Silcoon and Cascoon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It never forgets any attack it endured while in the cocoon. After evolution, it seeks payback.

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Butterfly Edge
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It has an aggressive nature. It stabs prey with its long, narrow mouth to drain the prey's fluids.

attacks.name: Cocoon Collector
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 4 in any combination of Silcoon and Cascoon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It drinks dew that collects on its silk and waits for evolution. Its hard cocoon repels attacks.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It lives among the tall grass and in forests. It repels attacks by raising up the spikes on its rear.
It lives among the tall grass and in forests. It repels attacks by raising up the spikes on its rear.

attacks.name: Mach Cut
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leaf Cyclone
attacks.effect: Move a Grass Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Jungle Heal-GX
attacks.effect: Heal all damage from each of your Pokémon that has any Grass Energy attached to it. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Grass Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Small hooks on the bottom of its feet catch on walls and ceilings. That is how it can hang from above.

attacks.name: Time Distortion
attacks.effect: Devolve any number of your Benched Pokémon as many times as you like. Put each Evolution card removed this way into your hand.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
flavorText: It has the power to travel across time, but it is said to appear only in peaceful times.

attacks.name: Powerful Friends
attacks.effect: If you have any Stage 2 Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: With its Herculean powers, it can easily throw around an object that is 100 times its own weight.

attacks.name: Triple Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 3 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Wrap-GX
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Drink
attacks.effect: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It stores berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.

attacks.name: Continuous Headbutt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It looks just like a pinecone. Its shell protects it from bird Pokémon that peck it by mistake.

attacks.name: Lost March
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon, except ◇ (Prism Star) Pokémon, in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Even in the fiercest wind, it can control its fluff to make its way to any place in the world it wants.
It spreads its petals to absorb sunlight. It also floats in the air to get closer to the sun.
It drifts on winds. It is said that when Hoppip gather in fields and mountains, spring is on the way.

attacks.name: Multiply
attacks.effect: Search your deck for Hoppip and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It drifts on winds. It is said that when Hoppip gather in fields and mountains, spring is on the way.

attacks.name: Trapping Thread
attacks.effect: Whenever your opponent plays an Item or Supporter card from their hand during their next turn, prevent all effects of that card done to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spins thread from both its rear and its mouth. Then it wraps its prey up in thread and sips their bodily fluids at its leisure.

attacks.name: Spider Scram
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. Put this Pokémon and all cards attached to it in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Some fishermen weave its sturdy thread into nets to catch fish Pokémon.
Meganium's breath has the power to revive dead grass and plants. It can make them healthy again.

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The buds that ring its neck give off a spicy aroma that perks people up.

attacks.name: Mini Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity. It loves to sunbathe.

attacks.name: Synthesis
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity. It loves to sunbathe.

attacks.name: Grip and Squeeze
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: One solid blow from its horns is enough to split apart a large tree. Its greatest rival in Alola is Vikavolt.

attacks.name: Fury Cutter
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 50 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: While young, they live together deep in the mountains, training themselves in how to fight with their scythes and move at high speeds.

attacks.name: Hefty Whip
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 130 more damage.

flavorText: Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can't even see its eyes.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It tangles any moving thing with its vines. Their subtle shaking is ticklish if you get ensnared.
